title: AIAM-1 Intent Claim
description: Structured assertion of purpose produced by an AI agent at the moment of action. Intent claims are not independently signed — they are signed as part of the attestation record (ATTESTATION §3.3). This is by design: the governance system attests to the intent, not the agent.

spec_version.description: AIAM-1 specification version this intent claim was authored against.

intent_id.description: Unique identifier for this intent claim. MUST be globally unique — use namespace+timestamp or UUID-based patterns. Reuse across action proposals is prohibited (INTENT §6.2).

goal_ref.description: Reference to the goal context in the agent's identity claim. MUST resolve to an active goal_id. Format enforced to match identity_claim goal_context.goal_id pattern.

action_ref.description: Reference to the action proposal this intent claim accompanies. MUST resolve to a pending action proposal. Format compatible with AGP-1 action_id.

action_proposal_timestamp.description: Timestamp of the action proposal this intent accompanies. Included to enable runtime tolerance checking (INT-003): conformant implementations reject intent claims whose timestamp diverges from this value by more than a configured tolerance window.

trigger.description: Structured trigger that prompted the agent to consider this action.

alternatives_considered.description: Other actions the agent evaluated before selecting this one. SHOULD in spec (INT-004), but when present MUST contain meaningful content (minLength: 10 per item, minItems: 1). An agent that always omits this field is a signal for behavioral consistency analysis.

selection_rationale.description: Why this action was selected over alternatives. Minimum 20 characters to prevent trivial entries.

extensions.description: Vendor-specific reasoning metadata, chain-of-thought traces for debugging, or forward-compatibility fields.

expected_outcome.description: Structured description of the expected result. This is the primary hook for outcome verification (INT-040.3) and the load-bearing field for intent spoofing detection. Structured as an object with result_type enum and target_state to enable machine comparison against actual outcomes.

dependency_refs.description: References to prior intent claims this action builds upon. Empty array if no dependencies. Items are intent_id references to enforce structural consistency. maxItems: 20 prevents pathological dependency chains that could attack evaluation performance.

timestamp.description: Time at which the intent claim was produced. MUST be at the moment of action, not synthesized after the fact (INT-003). Runtime tolerance checking compares this against action_proposal_timestamp.

confidence.description: Agent's self-assessed confidence in the action's alignment with the goal. SHOULD in spec — optional here. Two decimal places maximum (multipleOf: 0.01) to prevent false precision.

extensions.description: Vendor-specific or implementation-specific intent metadata. Use this for forward-compatible fields rather than expecting additionalProperties.
